medications.indication_rationale: Sun protection is the mainstay of melasma management and prevents worsening.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply every morning, reapply with sun exposure

medications.drug_name: topical lightening agent (e.g., hydroquinone) — elective, via GP/dermatology
medications.indication_rationale: Optional elective treatment to fade pigment; discussed at routine review, not urgent.
Skin irritation can worsen melasma pigmentation — avoid.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Daily SPF 50+, a wide-brimmed hat outdoors, and avoid harsh scrubs/peels.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: UV protection and avoiding irritation allow pigment to fade and prevent worsening.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use slow breathing to settle the acute panic; understand this is benign.

patient_education_points.point: This is melasma — a common, completely benign skin pigmentation driven by hormones (your new pill) and sun. It is NOT skin cancer.
Daily SPF 50+ and a hat are the most important treatments; avoid scrubbing the skin. We can discuss your pill and optional lightening creams at a routine review.
Keep up routine skin checks; see a doctor if any single spot becomes asymmetric, raised or changing, or if you feel systemically unwell with dizziness or vomiting.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: OCP review/alternatives and optional lightening therapy; routine skin check.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A single changing/atypical pigmented lesion, or systemic features (weakness, dizziness, vomiting) — seek review.

must_include_items: Diagnose benign melasma AFTER screening for a single atypical lesion (melanoma) and Addison's
Reassure clearly and support the acute anxiety (no ED needed)
Strict daily sun protection and avoid skin irritation
OCP review and a skin-check safety-net
acceptable_alternatives: Elective topical lightening therapy via GP
errors_of_commission: Sending benign symmetrical melasma to ED for 'skin cancer'
Ordering an adrenal work-up without indication
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires screened reassurance for benign melasma, sun protection, anxiety support and an appropriate skin-check safety-net; over-escalation or unscreened reassurance are failures.
